ranges.label: Lower Normal Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness level is within the normal range, indicating adequate sleep quality and duration. You appear to maintain good alertness throughout typical daily activities.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current sleep hygiene habits and consistent sleep-wake schedule
Stay physically active during the day to support restorative sleep quality at night
Avoid excessive caffeine intake in the afternoon and evening hours

ranges.label: Higher Normal Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is at the higher end of the normal range. While not clinically significant, this level suggests room for improvement in sleep habits or overall sleep quality.
suggestions: Ensure you are consistently getting 7-9 hours of quality sleep each night
Limit exposure to electronic screens at least one hour before bedtime to support natural melatonin production
Evaluate whether stress, irregular work schedules, or lifestyle factors may be affecting your sleep quality
Consult a healthcare provider if daytime sleepiness begins to interfere with your daily performance or safety

ranges.label: Mild Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is mildly elevated above the normal range. This may indicate insufficient sleep duration, poor sleep quality, or an underlying sleep disturbance that warrants attention.
suggestions: Keep a detailed sleep diary for at least two weeks to identify patterns in your sleep quality and duration
Evaluate and reduce factors that may impair sleep such as caffeine, alcohol, heavy meals before bed, and irregular schedules
Create an optimal sleep environment by ensuring your bedroom is dark, quiet, cool, and free from electronic distractions
Consider consulting a sleep medicine specialist if sleepiness persists despite improving sleep habits
Avoid napping for more than 20 minutes during the day as prolonged naps can disrupt nighttime sleep architecture

ranges.label: Moderate Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is moderately elevated, suggesting a possible underlying sleep disorder. Medical evaluation is recommended as this level of sleepiness may impair alertness during critical activities.
suggestions: Schedule an appointment with a sleep medicine specialist for a thorough clinical evaluation
A polysomnography or home sleep apnea test may be recommended to assess for conditions like obstructive sleep apnea
Exercise caution when driving or operating machinery, as your level of sleepiness may impair reaction time and judgment
Discuss with your physician whether any current medications may be contributing to your excessive daytime sleepiness

ranges.label: Severe Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is severely elevated, strongly suggesting an underlying sleep disorder such as sleep apnea or narcolepsy. Prompt medical evaluation is essential for your health and safety.
suggestions: Seek urgent evaluation from a sleep medicine specialist to identify the cause of your severe daytime sleepiness
Undergo a comprehensive sleep study including polysomnography and potentially a multiple sleep latency test
Avoid driving, operating heavy machinery, or performing safety-critical tasks until you have been properly diagnosed and treated
Inform household members and close contacts about your condition so they can provide support and monitor for safety concerns
Ask your doctor about interim management strategies while awaiting definitive diagnosis and treatment

ranges.label: Low Meaning in Life
ranges.description: Your overall meaning in life score is low, suggesting limited perceived purpose and relatively little active exploration of meaning. This pattern may be associated with feelings of emptiness or disengagement.
suggestions: Begin exploring your personal values by reflecting on activities and relationships that have felt most meaningful to you in the past
Engage in volunteering, community service, or helping others, as prosocial behavior is strongly linked to increased sense of purpose
Share your thoughts and feelings with trusted friends, family members, or a counselor to gain new perspectives on your life direction
Try journaling about moments when you feel most alive and engaged, as these can reveal sources of latent meaning

ranges.label: Moderate Meaning in Life
ranges.description: You have a moderate sense of meaning in life and are somewhat engaged in exploring your purpose. Some areas of your life likely feel meaningful while others may still be developing or uncertain.
suggestions: Continue exploring what matters most to you by trying new activities, deepening existing interests, and reflecting on your core values
Create a personal mission statement or list of life priorities to clarify and strengthen your sense of direction
Set specific goals that align with your identified values, and track your progress toward achieving them over time

ranges.label: High Meaning in Life
ranges.description: You have a strong sense of meaning and purpose in your life. You clearly understand what matters to you and are actively living in alignment with your values and goals.
suggestions: Continue living intentionally and nurturing the relationships, activities, and commitments that give your life meaning
Consider mentoring or supporting others in discovering their own sense of purpose, as sharing wisdom deepens personal meaning
Remain open to growth and new experiences, as meaning can evolve and deepen through life transitions and fresh challenges

presence.label: Low Presence
presence.description: You currently experience little felt sense of meaning in your life. Daily activities may feel purposeless or disconnected from your deeper values and aspirations.

presence.label: Moderate Presence
presence.description: You have a moderate sense of meaning in your life. You sometimes feel purposeful and connected to what matters, but this experience may be inconsistent across situations.

presence.label: High Presence
presence.description: You clearly and consistently feel that your life is meaningful. You have a strong sense of purpose that provides direction and satisfaction in your daily experience.

search.label: Low Search
search.description: You are not actively searching for meaning in your life. This may reflect contentment with your current sense of purpose or a lack of motivation to explore existential questions.

search.label: Moderate Search
search.description: You are moderately engaged in exploring the meaning of your life. You occasionally reflect on purpose and direction, balancing curiosity with acceptance of your current situation.

search.label: High Search
search.description: You are actively and intensely searching for meaning and purpose in your life. This strong drive to explore existential questions may reflect a transitional period or deep philosophical engagement.

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, indicating no significant autism spectrum traits. Your social communication, attention flexibility, and imaginative abilities appear typical.
suggestions: This result suggests you are unlikely to be on the autism spectrum based on this brief screening measure
If you continue to have specific concerns about social interaction or sensory processing, consider discussing them with a healthcare professional
Remember that this is a screening tool and does not replace comprehensive clinical evaluation

ranges.label: Borderline Range
ranges.description: Your score is in the borderline range, suggesting some possible autism spectrum traits in areas such as social communication or attention switching. This does not constitute a diagnosis.
suggestions: This borderline score does not imply a diagnosis but may warrant further self-observation and professional consultation
If these traits cause difficulties in your social relationships, work, or daily routines, consider seeking a comprehensive evaluation
Learning about autism spectrum conditions and neurodiversity can help with greater self-understanding and acceptance
Consider speaking with a psychologist or psychiatrist who specializes in adult autism spectrum assessment

ranges.label: Further Assessment Recommended
ranges.description: Your score is elevated, suggesting notable autism spectrum traits across multiple domains. A comprehensive professional assessment is recommended to determine whether these traits meet diagnostic criteria.
suggestions: Contact a clinical psychologist or psychiatrist experienced in autism spectrum assessment for a comprehensive diagnostic evaluation
This screening result is not a diagnosis and requires in-depth clinical assessment including developmental history and behavioral observation
Understanding your unique cognitive and social profile can help you develop personalized strategies for managing daily challenges
Explore local autism support resources, online communities, and advocacy organizations that can provide information and peer support
Regardless of the diagnostic outcome, identifying your strengths and challenges can improve self-awareness and quality of life

ranges.label: Low Risk
ranges.description: You reported few psychotic-like experiences, indicating no elevated risk for psychosis at this time. Your perceptual and cognitive experiences appear to be within the normal range.
suggestions: No particular clinical concern is indicated at this time based on your screening results
If you experience any persistent unusual perceptions, intrusive thoughts, or feelings of unreality in the future, consider consulting a professional
Maintain healthy lifestyle habits including adequate sleep, regular exercise, and stress management as general protective factors

ranges.label: Attention Needed
ranges.description: You reported several psychotic-like experiences, placing you near the clinical screening threshold. While not necessarily pathological, these experiences should be monitored for changes in frequency or intensity.
suggestions: Pay close attention to the frequency, intensity, and duration of any unusual perceptual or cognitive experiences over the coming weeks
Maintain regular daily routines, consistent sleep schedules, and meaningful social connections as these support psychological stability
Minimize or eliminate use of psychoactive substances including cannabis and excessive alcohol, as these can exacerbate psychotic-like experiences
If these experiences increase in frequency, become more distressing, or begin to affect your daily functioning, consult a psychiatrist promptly

ranges.label: Screen Positive
ranges.description: Your score exceeds the clinical screening threshold, suggesting possible ultra-high risk for psychosis. This is a screening result, not a diagnosis, and requires professional clinical evaluation for proper interpretation.
suggestions: Consider scheduling an appointment with a psychiatrist or psychologist for a comprehensive clinical evaluation of your experiences
Early identification and intervention for psychosis risk have strong evidence for improving long-term outcomes and preventing progression
Maintain a healthy lifestyle including regular sleep patterns, balanced nutrition, physical activity, and supportive social relationships
Completely avoid all psychoactive substances including cannabis, stimulants, and hallucinogens as these can significantly increase psychosis risk
Share your experiences with a trusted family member or friend who can provide emotional support and help monitor any changes

ranges.label: Highly Positive
ranges.description: You reported many psychotic-like experiences, indicating a substantially elevated screening score. A comprehensive psychiatric evaluation is strongly recommended, as early intervention can significantly improve outcomes.
suggestions: Strongly recommend scheduling an appointment with a psychiatrist as soon as possible for a thorough clinical assessment
Early intervention services specializing in psychosis risk can provide structured professional support and monitoring tailored to your needs
Inform trusted family members or close friends about your experiences so they can offer support and assist with accessing appropriate care
Remember that endorsing these experiences on a screening questionnaire does not mean you have a psychotic disorder, and many people with elevated scores do not develop psychosis
